_meta.about: The Maker Catalog = the MENU of what THIS CLASS OF MACHINE CAN MAKE. Rule: if the synth cannot make it, it is NOT a catalog item. Things the machine fundamentally cannot make (a working phone, matter from energy) are NOT listed here - the checker still recognises such requests and honestly DECLINES them, routing to docs/north-star.md. Every item decomposes into its real substance (components -> molecules -> atoms; DNA/proteins where biological), gets a feasibility verdict, and routes to a maker. Catalog feasibility: ready (a maker in this repo makes it today) > assemble (a cheap real-world maker - kitchen/3D-print - makes it; integration planned) > frontier (synth-makeable in principle, demonstrated in a lab; on the ladder, docs/the-ladder.md). Extend by adding a makeable item below.

items.name: water

styles.how: Peltier condenses water from humid air (AWG); carbon filter + UV-C makes it potable; set temperature; dispense
treat + temperature + dispense from a pre-filled tank (instant)

items.notes: Two styles, one system - the literal 'same machine, different methods' case.

styles.how: harvest/treat water then chill the plate below 0 C

items.name: coffee
decomposition: water + coffee solubles (caffeine, chlorogenic acids, melanoidins)

styles.how: pump water + coffee concentrate in ratio; set hot/iced

items.notes: Assembled from stocked concentrate, not synthesised from atoms.

styles.how: compile shape -> scaffold + staples + protocol -> fold + gel-verify on the rig
fold a DNA origami, then sol-gel silicify (TEOS + aminosilane) into rigid SiO2

items.name: functionalized nanostructure
decomposition: a nm shape carrying guests at chosen sites

styles.how: compile with --decorate/--cascade to place guests at nm sites via handle/anti-handle
styles.citation: Fu et al., JACS 134:5516 (2012); Kuzyk et al., Nature 483:311 (2012)

items.notes: Rung 2: a shape that does chemistry. Positions a guest you supply.

items.name: gold nanostructure

styles.how: design a DNA-origami cavity mold + gold seed, grow metal to cast the shape
styles.citation: Sun et al., Science 346:1258361 (2014); Helmi et al., Nano Lett. 14:6693 (2014)

items.notes: Demonstrated in labs; QC needs TEM - a stretch, not desktop-turnkey yet.

items.name: cookie
decomposition: (wheat/egg DNA present in ingredients but NOT what makes a cookie a cookie)

styles.how: dose stocked ingredients, mix, deposit, bake - automatable (food 3D printers + a heater)
styles.citation: food 3D-printing (e.g., extrusion + IR/laser bake) is demonstrated commercially

items.notes: His point exactly: a cookie is ingredients -> molecules -> atoms. We assemble + bake from stock; we do not synthesise flour from atoms.

items.name: bread
items.notes: Time + temperature program over stocked ingredients.

items.name: printed object

styles.how: printsynth: STL -> print job (material, settings, time/filament/cost estimate) -> standard FDM printer + free slicer

items.notes: Form in plastic, single material class. Not functional electronics. A real maker now (synth/printsynth).

items.name: enzyme

styles.how: cell-free transcription/translation (PURE/TXTL) of a DNA template, or order the protein
styles.citation: Shimizu et al., Nat. Biotechnol. 19:751 (2001) (PURE system)

items.notes: Demonstrated; not a $1500 desktop yet. The DNA->protein step is real but lab-grade.

items.name: small molecule

styles.how: DNA-templated synthesis or an artificial molecular assembler directs the reactions

items.notes: Programmable molecular synthesis is demonstrated for specific cases; general/scalable is rung 5+.
